objects.name: Analytic 1741
objects.description: The defender correlates app-driven shell or command execution setup with subsequent process creation, command invocation, or script-driven follow-on behavior under the same app context, especially when command execution occurs from background state, without recent user interaction, or immediately after payload retrieval or local staging. The analytic prioritizes Android-observable control-plane effects: Java Runtime or similar command-execution method use, shell or sh-like process creation, command parameter visibility where available, and immediate file or network effects produced by the interpreter.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application invokes Runtime.exec, ProcessBuilder, JNI-backed command launcher, or equivalent command-execution bridge immediately before shell or command process creation

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application spawns shell, command interpreter, or command-executing child process with arguments during command-execution phase

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between command-launch method use, process creation, and follow-on file or network effects
Apps legitimately expected to run shell-like or administrative commands, such as enterprise support tools, terminal apps, approved EMM agents, or developer tooling
Expected command interpreters, process names, or parent-child execution chains for approved apps
Whether command execution should occur only during active user-driven workflows
Environment-specific list of suspicious command arguments, redirection usage, chaining operators, or shell-control syntax
Minimum number or size of file artifacts created after interpreter execution to increase confidence
Minimum outbound volume after command execution to treat network behavior as meaningful
